evals.description: Trigger test: activates on compensation benchmarking question
evals.prompt: We're hiring a Staff Engineer and I want to make sure our offer is competitive. How do I benchmark compensation for this role?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on equity grant question
evals.prompt: We're a Series B startup about to make our first Staff Engineer hire. What's a reasonable equity grant and how should we structure it?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on leveling framework question
evals.prompt: Our engineering team has grown to 40 people and we don't have formal levels. People are asking how to get promoted and we have no answer. Where do we start?

evals.description: Core task: designing pay bands with correct spread and overlap
evals.prompt: I need to create salary bands for our 4-level IC engineering track. We want to target P50 base. Walk me through how to structure the bands including spread and overlap.

evals.description: Core task: explains total rewards beyond base salary
evals.prompt: A candidate turned us down saying our base was lower than another offer, but we think our total package is actually better. How do I make the case for total compensation?

evals.description: Core task: handling pay compression correctly
evals.prompt: A new engineer we just hired is being paid more than a senior engineer who has been with us for 3 years. How do I handle this?

evals.description: Core task: structuring equity refreshes for retention
evals.prompt: We gave early employees big equity grants 3 years ago. Most of their grants are nearly fully vested and we're worried they'll leave once the 4-year mark hits. What should we do?

evals.description: Core task: running a pay equity audit
evals.prompt: We're a team of 80 and our Head of People wants to run our first pay equity audit. What does the process look like and what do we do with the results?

evals.description: Core task: writing a compensation philosophy
evals.prompt: Employees keep asking why they're paid what they're paid and how to earn more. We don't have a formal compensation philosophy. What should it cover?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against setting pay from last person's salary
evals.prompt: We're opening a backend engineer role. The last person in this seat was making $120k when they left. Should we just use that as the starting point for the offer range?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against single-trigger equity acceleration
evals.prompt: We're drafting equity agreements for our new VP hire. They're asking for acceleration on change of control. Should we agree to single-trigger or double-trigger acceleration?

evals.description: References load: points to equity-guide for ISO vs RSU decision
evals.prompt: We're moving from ISO grants to RSUs as we approach our Series D. Our employees are confused about the difference and what it means for their taxes. Where should I point them?
